other_observers: Nick Moore, who agreed with ID
description: Large gull, larger than nearby Herring Gulls, with thick-chested, short-winged stout appearance. Stood taller and looked bulkier than its neighboring HERGs. Plumage uniformly grayish brown, moderately pale (somewhat paler than first cycle HERGs nearby), with overall smudgy aspect to plumage. Back, wing-coverts, tertials, and primaries all appeared to be of the same color, as were the head, chest, and underparts. -- more like juvenile than a full-on first-winter plumage GWGU.  Bill all black. Description written within 5 minutes of seeing bird
